<<<<<<< HEAD rewrite on Yukai Wang https://yukiiwong.github.io/tags/rewrite/ Recent content in rewrite on Yukai Wang ======= rewrite on Eddie Webb https://yukiiwong.github.io/tags/rewrite/ Recent content in rewrite on Eddie Webb >>>>>>> 9983a5d67df7424dd253ffe5acb5e9c598f38bdc Hugo -- gohugo.io en-us Thu, 01 Jan 2009 14:09:10 +0000
Forcing Visits to use SSL <<<<<<< HEAD https://yukiiwong.github.io/more/force-ssl/ Thu, 01 Jan 2009 14:09:10 +0000
https://yukiiwong.github.io/more/force-ssl/ ======= https://yukiiwong.github.io/blog/force-ssl/ Thu, 01 Jan 2009 14:09:10 +0000
https://yukiiwong.github.io/blog/force-ssl/ >>>>>>> 9983a5d67df7424dd253ffe5acb5e9c598f38bdc Intro Doesn’t matter whether it’s a CakePHP app for a client, your own personal CMS, or any other web based application. If your passing around passwords or other sensitive info you should really implement SSL. SSL provides 2 main perks to your visitors. First it encrypts all communication that flies across the web. This prevents curious or devious billies from getting your secrets. Secondly it ensures to the user that your server is in fact who it claims, and not a nasty ‘man in the middle" attack.
